next_cursor.reason: Refresh SOSP/OSDI/ATC/FAST venue readiness against the same frozen worktree and evidence map.

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: Gate 0.14 behavior-equivalence closeout after decomposition

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: paper negative guard for clean-module claims

objections.negative_guard: No production-clean, modular-storage-runtime, or behavior-equivalent refactor claim before Gate 0.14 closes.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Implementation text may describe current modules only as refactored components after behavior-equivalence evidence; until then, it must not claim a production-clean architecture.
objections.reviewer_objection: The current filesystem path still needs a defensible storage-runtime architecture, not a set of accumulated FUSE, CUDA, TPM, and checklist patches.
objections.title: codebase architecture still looks patch-accumulated

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: filesystem_viability_breakdown end-to-end component attribution

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: strict-versus-epoch lock-hold and p99/p99.9 closeout

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: paper text classifying the dominant bottleneck

objections.negative_guard: No high-throughput, competitive-with-gocryptfs, or general-purpose filesystem wording before viability breakdown and epoch evidence close.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: The paper may report conservative strict-mode results and epoch-mode diagnostics, but no high-throughput or general-purpose filesystem implication is allowed until A1/A3 close.
objections.reviewer_objection: AEGIS-Q frozen-contract throughput is far below plaintext and gocryptfs, and the paper must explain whether the bottleneck is semantic, implementation, or redesign debt.
objections.title: filesystem throughput viability remains the top blocker

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: matched fscrypt fio row or environment-blocked proof

objections.negative_guard: No fscrypt speedup or apples-to-apples implication without a measured fscrypt row.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: fscrypt must appear only as measured, environment-blocked, or removed from the comparison.
objections.reviewer_objection: fscrypt is missing from the matched frozen-contract baseline table.
objections.title: fscrypt matched baseline is unresolved

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: LUKS/dmsetup setup logs and matched fio JSON for dm-crypt/ext4

objections.negative_guard: Historical dm-crypt diagnostics are not a paper comparison row.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: dm-crypt is either a measured row or an unavailable row with root/device proof.
objections.reviewer_objection: dm-crypt appears runnable but lacks a matched frozen-contract row.
objections.title: dm-crypt matched baseline is unresolved

missing_artifacts.label: QoS repeated report
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/reports/qos_repeated/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: at least two cgroup/blk-throttle/ionice/BFQ baseline runs

objections.negative_guard: No SQLite p99 uniqueness claim before two kernel QoS baselines exist.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: SQLite p99 improvement must be compared against kernel QoS or scoped as internal controller evidence.
objections.reviewer_objection: SQLite p99 recovery may be achievable with standard Linux QoS controls.
objections.title: SQLite QoS lacks kernel-control baselines

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: paper POSIX envelope table with support/formal rejection/paper limitation

objections.negative_guard: No general-purpose POSIX wording before shared mmap and POSIX envelope evidence close.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: The paper must read as a scoped FUSE secure-storage prototype unless shared mmap and envelope semantics pass.
objections.reviewer_objection: Shared mmap, rename, directory fsync, and concurrent writes are not closed enough for broad POSIX wording.
objections.title: POSIX envelope is too broad unless closed by tests or formal rejection

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: physical power-loss or explicit unavailable-environment boundary artifact

objections.negative_guard: No power-loss safe, full crash certification, or crash-certified wording without physical or explicitly bounded evidence.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Crash claims must be bounded to the strongest retained model: process kill, daemon kill, remount, or physical power cut.
objections.reviewer_objection: SIGKILL-at-cutpoint evidence is not the same as power-loss certification.
objections.title: crash and power-loss model is not fully bounded

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: production async Merkle or committed-prefix root plus TPM epoch evidence

objections.negative_guard: No TPM rollback resistance, persistent PCR-bound freshness, sealed-key release, or full rollback-resistance wording before Gate C6.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Freshness text must map to the retained ladder level and stop before PCR-bound rollback resistance unless C6 exists.
objections.reviewer_objection: File anchors and TPM NV evidence do not prove persistent PCR-bound rollback resistance.
objections.title: freshness and rollback resistance remain overclaim-prone

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: Argon2id/scrypt implementation or PBKDF2 parameter/salt/offline-attack benchmark artifact

objections.negative_guard: No offline-attack resistance or strong password-hardening claim while PBKDF2 remains unexplained.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Security text must state the KDF, salt, parameters, offline attacker model, and PQC/data-plane separation.
objections.reviewer_objection: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 and key lifecycle boundaries need a parameterized decision backed by artifacts.
objections.title: KDF and key lifecycle need a security-facing decision

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: production mounted-path benefit traces before any NVIDIA term becomes a paper mechanism

objections.negative_guard: No NVMe-to-UVM, GPUDirect, dma-buf zero-copy, CUDA isolation, or stream-priority QoS claim without production evidence.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: NVIDIA terms may appear only at their retained evidence level: API available, probe passed, diagnostic, or non-claim.
objections.reviewer_objection: Pinned memory, UVM, dma-buf, GPUDirect, stream priority, and Jetson power claims need local probes, production traces, and official-document boundaries.
objections.title: GPU and Jetson optimization claims need a ladder

missing_artifacts.label: hero result contract
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/hero_result_contract/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: second macrobenchmark
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/second_macrobenchmark/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: RocksDB/WAL, checkpointing, or secure inference-log macrobenchmark runner and raw logs

objections.negative_guard: No broad workload-general or SOSP/OSDI-ready claim before a second macrobenchmark exists.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: SQLite can remain the primary hero, but generality requires a second macrobenchmark or downgraded venue ambition.
objections.reviewer_objection: SQLite/FUSE QoS alone is not enough for SOSP/OSDI workload generality.
objections.title: hero workload generality is too narrow

missing_artifacts.label: first two pages audit
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/first_two_pages_thesis_audit/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: recurring review audit
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/recurring_review_elimination_audit/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: paper text pass with dangerous phrases absent, negated, or evidence-backed

objections.negative_guard: Dangerous phrases must be absent, explicitly negated, or tied to a closed gate.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Paper body must prioritize thesis, hard problem, mechanism, result, and loss case, not artifact-ledger narration.
objections.reviewer_objection: The paper can sound broader than the retained production evidence.
objections.title: paper narrative can overclaim the evidence

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: vfs_ebpf_fdatasync_storm context-switch/syscall/fdatasync/tail-latency breakdown

objections.negative_guard: No eBPF passthrough, io_uring completion bypass, or async epoch fdatasync claim without production evidence.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: The design may claim fdatasync amortization only for the measured epoch path; eBPF/io_uring bypass remains a non-claim.
objections.reviewer_objection: FUSE context switching and per-write fdatasync can dominate the path and must be amortized or honestly measured.
objections.title: FUSE and fdatasync storm need amortization or measured rejection

missing_artifacts.label: side-channel diagnostics
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/sidechannel_eval/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: TVLA diagnostics
missing_artifacts.path: artifacts/validation/tvla_leakage_eval/*.json

missing_artifacts.label: required closure artifact
missing_artifacts.path: claim lint output proving out-of-scope language remains explicit

objections.negative_guard: No side-channel protection, multi-tenant defense, compromised-kernel defense, deployment-ready, or ready-for-production wording.
objections.paper_claim_boundary: Security scope must exclude compromised kernel/driver/FUSE, privileged local attackers, multi-tenant isolation, and GPU side-channel defense.
objections.reviewer_objection: GPU side channels, multi-tenant attackers, privileged local attackers, and deployment readiness are not implemented defenses.
objections.title: attacker model and deployment claims must stay scoped

scope: Gate 0.2-S0 recurring review-objection map against the frozen worktree and active gates.
